 {"id":"bd-ix9c","title":"Implement run_sequential_agent_sweep()","description":"# Sequential Agent Sweep Implementation

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Process repositories one at a time (default mode, safest).

## Implementation

```bash
run_sequential_agent_sweep() {
    local -n repos_ref=$1
    local with_release="$2"
    local success_count=0
    local fail_count=0
    
    for repo_spec in "${repos_ref[@]}"; do
        local repo_name repo_path session_name
        repo_name=$(basename "$repo_spec" | sed "s/@.*//")
        repo_path=$(repo_spec_to_path "$repo_spec")
        session_name="ru_sweep_${repo_name//[^a-zA-Z0-9_]/_}_$$"
        
        log_step "Processing: $repo_name"
        
        # Run preflight check
        if \! repo_preflight_check "$repo_path"; then
            log_warn "  Skipping: $PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"
            write_result "$repo_name" "agent-sweep" "preflight_failed" "0" "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON" "$repo_path"
            continue
        fi
        
        if run_single_agent_workflow "$session_name" "$repo_path" "$with_release"; then
            log_success "  Completed: $repo_name"
            ((success_count++))
            mark_repo_completed "$repo_spec"
        else
            log_error "  Failed: $repo_name"
            ((fail_count++))
        fi
        
        save_agent_sweep_state "in_progress"
    done
    
    SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT=$success_count
    SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT=$fail_count
    
    [[ $fail_count -gt 0 ]] && return 1
    return 0
}
```

## Features
- Preflight check before each repo
- State saved after each repo (for resume)
- Clear progress logging
- Counts success/fail for summary","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:57:06.003069108-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T23:37:55.273510603-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T23:37:55.273510603-05:00","close_reason":"Function fully implemented with progress tracking, success/failure recording, and proper error handling","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix9c","depends_on_id":"bd-b00c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:47.557304053-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ix9c","depends_on_id":"bd-51fm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:47.585784474-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ix9c","depends_on_id":"bd-hkmt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:47.606664655-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ix9c","depends_on_id":"bd-yrod","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:55:16.040252145-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-jen3","title":"Implement review command exit codes and error classification","description":"# Task: Implement Review Command Exit Codes

## Purpose
Define and implement consistent exit codes for `ru review` following the ru exit code conventions, enabling automation and scripting.

## Background: ru Exit Code Convention
From AGENTS.md, ru uses these exit codes:
- 0 = Success (all repos synced/reviewed)
- 1 = Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2 = Conflicts exist (needs resolution)
- 3 = Dependency/system error (gh missing, auth failed)
- 4 = Invalid arguments
- 5 = Interrupted (use --resume)

## Review-Specific Exit Codes

### Exit 0: Success
- All sessions completed successfully
- All questions answered
- All approved changes applied (if --apply)

### Exit 1: Partial Failure
- Some sessions failed but others succeeded
- Some repos could not be reviewed (network, rate limit)
- Partial progress was made

### Exit 2: Conflicts/Review Needed
- Agent detected merge conflicts in worktree
- Quality gates failed (tests/lint)
- Changes require human intervention before apply

### Exit 3: Dependency/System Error
- Claude Code not installed or not in PATH
- gh CLI not authenticated
- tmux not available (for local driver)
- ntm not available (when --mode=ntm specified)

### Exit 4: Invalid Arguments
- Unknown flags
- Invalid --mode value
- Invalid --priority value
- Conflicting options (--plan with --push)

### Exit 5: Interrupted
- SIGINT/SIGTERM received
- --max-runtime exceeded
- --max-questions exceeded
- Checkpoint saved, use --resume to continue

## Implementation

### Error Classification Function
```bash
classify_review_error() {
    local error_type="$1"
    local context="$2"
    
    case "$error_type" in
        session_failed|rate_limited|network_error)
            echo "partial"  # Exit 1
            ;;
        merge_conflict|quality_gate_failed|tests_failed)
            echo "conflict"  # Exit 2
            ;;
        missing_dependency|auth_failed|no_driver)
            echo "system"  # Exit 3
            ;;
        invalid_flag|bad_mode|conflicting_options)
            echo "invalid"  # Exit 4
            ;;
        interrupted|timeout|max_questions)
            echo "interrupted"  # Exit 5
            ;;
        *)
            echo "unknown"
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Exit Code Aggregation
```bash
aggregate_exit_code() {
    local -a exit_codes=("$@")
    local max_code=0
    
    for code in "${exit_codes[@]}"; do
        [[ $code -gt $max_code ]] && max_code=$code
    done
    
    # Special case: 5 (interrupted) takes precedence over 1 (partial)
    if [[ " ${exit_codes[*]} " =~ " 5 " ]]; then
        echo 5
        return
    fi
    
    echo $max_code
}
```

### Final Exit Handling
```bash
finalize_review_exit() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    
    case "$exit_code" in
        0) log_success "Review completed successfully" ;;
        1) log_warn "Review completed with partial failures" ;;
        2) log_error "Review blocked by conflicts - manual resolution needed" ;;
        3) log_error "Review failed due to system/dependency error" ;;
        4) log_error "Invalid arguments" ;;
        5) log_warn "Review interrupted - use --resume to continue" ;;
    esac
    
    exit "$exit_code"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify each exit code for its conditions
- Verify --resume works after exit 5
- Verify aggregation chooses worst code
- Verify CI can parse exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 exit codes defined and documented
- [ ] Error classification works correctly
- [ ] Exit code aggregation from multiple sessions
- [ ] Clear log message for each exit code
- [ ] --resume only available after exit 5

 {"id":"bd-jk4n","title":"[EPIC] Security Guardrails & Validation","description":"# Security Guardrails & Validation

## Critical Insight
The prompt "Don't commit ephemeral files" is NOT sufficient. The agent can ignore it.
ru MUST enforce guardrails before executing any commit/push.

## Planner → Validator → Executor Model
1. Agent produces structured JSON plans (commits, releases)
2. ru validates plans against security rules
3. ru executes only validated plans
4. Agent NEVER directly runs git commands

## File Denylist
Before executing commit plan, validate each file against denylist:
- .env, .env.*
- *.pem, *.key, id_rsa, *.p12, *.pfx
- credentials.json, secrets.json
- node_modules, __pycache__, .pyc
- dist/, build/, *.log, .DS_Store

## File Size Limits
Default: 10MB max per file (configurable via AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB)

## Binary Detection
Detect binary files and require explicit allow:
- Uses `file -b` to check if file is text/script/json/xml

## Secret Scanning
Layered approach:
1. If gitleaks installed: Full scan
2. Elif detect-secrets installed: Scan
3. Else: Heuristic patterns (AWS keys, GitHub PATs, private keys, etc.)

Block push if secrets detected. Write artifact report. Mark repo failed.

## Commit Plan Validation
validate_commit_plan() checks:
- Files not on denylist
- Files under size limit
- No binaries without explicit allow
- Secret scan passes

## Release Plan Validation
- Version format valid
- Version files exist
- Tag format valid

## Policy
Default: Block commit on validation failure, preserve plan in artifacts for manual review.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:45:36.238165636-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:45:36.238165636-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jk4n","depends_on_id":"bd-bx6s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:20.089190203-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-jleo","title":"Fix get_repo_status rev-list failure output","description":"Problem: get_repo_status currently emits AHEAD=? BEHIND=? when git rev-list fails (e.g., unusual repo states). cmd_status JSON output prints ahead/behind as numbers; non-numeric values can produce invalid/misleading output and may print errors.

Fix:
- Ensure get_repo_status always outputs numeric ahead/behind (use -1/-1 for unknown).
- Avoid leaking a global output var by making it local.
- Add a regression test that forces rev-list failure via a git wrapper and asserts AHEAD/BEHIND are numeric.

Acceptance:
- ru status --json prints valid JSON even when rev-list fails.
- scripts/test_local_git.sh includes a passing regression test.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-05T15:17:58.069291577-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T15:18:08.327908375-05:00"}

Fix:
- Ensure get_repo_status always outputs numeric ahead/behind (use -1/-1 for unknown).
- Avoid leaking a global output var by making it local.
- Add a regression test that forces rev-list failure via a git wrapper and asserts AHEAD/BEHIND are numeric.

Acceptance:

 {"id":"bd-jm89","title":"Define unified session driver interface","description":"# Task: Define Unified Session Driver Interface

## Purpose
Create an abstract interface that both ntm and local drivers implement, enabling graceful degradation and consistent behavior regardless of which driver is used.

## Background: Why a Unified Interface?
- ntm provides advanced orchestration but may not be installed
- Local driver (tmux + stream-json) works everywhere
- Same code should work with either driver
- Enables testing without full ntm dependency
- Future drivers (remote, cloud) can plug in

## Interface Definition

### Driver Capabilities
```bash
# Query what driver can do
driver_capabilities() {
    # Returns JSON with capabilities
    cat << EOF
{
  "name": "local",
  "parallel_sessions": true,
  "activity_detection": false,
  "health_monitoring": false,
  "question_routing": true
}
EOF
}
```

### Core Operations
```bash
# Start a new Claude session
# Args: worktree_path, session_name, initial_prompt
# Returns: session_id
driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    # Implementation varies by driver
}

# Send message/answer to session
# Args: session_id, message
# Returns: 0 on success
driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
}

# Get current session state
# Args: session_id
# Returns: JSON with state info
driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    # Returns: {"state": "generating|waiting|complete|error", ...}
}

# Stop/kill a session
# Args: session_id
driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}

# Interrupt session (Ctrl+C equivalent)
# Args: session_id
driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}
```

### Event Streaming
```bash
# Stream events from session
# Args: session_id, callback_function
# Callback receives: event_type, event_data
driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    # Calls: $callback "question" "$question_json"
    # Calls: $callback "complete" "$status"
    # Calls: $callback "error" "$error_msg"
}
```

### Normalized Event Schema
Both drivers emit the same event format:
```json
{
  "type": "init|generating|waiting|complete|error",
  "session_id": "...",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
  "wait_info": {
    "reason": "ask_user_question|agent_question_text|external_prompt|unknown",
    "context": "...",
    "options": ["a) ...", "b) ..."],
    "recommended": "a",
    "risk_level": "low|medium|high"
  }
}
```

## Driver Selection
```bash
detect_review_driver() {
    # Try ntm first (preferred)
    if command -v ntm &>/dev/null; then
        if ntm --robot-status &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_verbose "Using ntm driver (robot mode available)"
            echo "ntm"
            return
        fi
        log_verbose "ntm found but robot mode unavailable"
    fi

    # Fall back to local driver
    if command -v tmux &>/dev/null && command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
        log_verbose "Using local driver (tmux + stream-json)"
        echo "local"
        return
    fi

    log_error "No suitable driver available"
    echo "unsupported"
}
```

## Driver Loading
```bash
load_review_driver() {
    local driver="$1"
    
    case "$driver" in
        ntm)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/ntm.sh"
            ;;
        local)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/local.sh"
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown driver: $driver"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
    
    REVIEW_DRIVER="$driver"
}
```

## Implementation Notes
- Functions prefixed with driver_ are implemented per-driver
- Common code calls driver_* functions
- Drivers can extend interface with driver-specific features
- Capabilities check enables graceful feature degradation

## Testing
- Mock driver for unit tests
- Verify interface consistency between drivers
- Test driver selection logic
- Verify event normalization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Interface defined with all required operations
- [ ] Driver selection works correctly
- [ ] Event schema documented and consistent
- [ ] Capabilities query implemented
- [ ] Driver loading mechanism works","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.284949263-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:39:51.577905888-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:39:51.577905888-05:00","close_reason":"Unified driver interface implemented with: load_review_driver(), driver_capabilities(), driver_start_session(), driver_send_to_session(), driver_get_session_state(), driver_stop_session(), driver_interrupt_session(), driver_stream_events(), driver_list_sessions(), driver_session_alive(). Includes event schema documentation.
